dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses false breakouts (new highs/lows) as confirmation. Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) over a 20-bar lookback to identify new highs/lows. Entry requires price closing beyond the breakout level (2 consecutive bars approximated by checking current close vs previous high/low). Stop loss set to 2× risk at 2.0% per exit condition ('2× risk stop loss'). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT because the signal can trigger either long (false high breakout) or short (false low breakout). Penetration threshold (0.5% of range) is implicit in Donchian breakout detection and not explicitly encoded as a separate condition.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses false breakouts (new highs/lows) as confirmation. Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) over a 20-bar lookback to identify new highs/lows. Entry requires price closing beyond the breakout level (2 consecutive bars approximated by checking current close vs previous high/low). Stop loss set to 2× risk at 2.0% per exit condition ('2× risk stop loss'). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT because the signal can trigger either long (false high breakout) or short (false low breakout). Penetration threshold (0.5% of range) is implicit in Donchian breakout detection and not explicitly encoded as a separate condition.

source_rule_text: A trader should wait for confirmation that an anticipated market move has actually begun before entering, rather than entering at the time of initial prediction; use false breakouts (moves to new highs/lows) as confirmation signals to set up trades.
